text:
The defining moment Zimbabwean constitutional reform came 2008 after the disputed and violence riddled elections 2008 when the three main political parties entered into transitional Government National Unity and were tasked with the establishment new constitution which was ultimately adopted 2013 following protracted and turbulent process which began 2009.
Some segments the civil society however argued that the concerned political parties had ‘captured the constitutional project and narrowed short-term struggle motivated the pursuit party political interests the expense the will the people and nation’s broad long-term interests’, and thereby subverted and/or negated the aspirations the people.
against this background that the study therefore assesses the participation, role and significance the rural populace the drafting the 2013 Zimbabwean Constitution.
The study traces the history constitutional reform efforts Zimbabwe, beginning with the colonial Lancaster House Constitution 1979, the protracted exercise 2009 2013 which gave birth the current Constitution.
focuses the 2009-13 constitution making process case study.
The study employs approach adopting both doctrinal and empirical research approaches.
The study employed the doctrinal research approach provide for doctrinal analysis the relevant global, regional and domestic legislation and case law.
The empirical research approach, through interviews, was used collect qualitative data from the general members the rural populace and key institutions such political parties and human rights organisations from three selected rural districts, namely Bulilima, Makonde and Mutasa.
The study indicated that while significant number the rural populace participated the constitution making process, the legal environment which subsisted during the constitution making process did not allow for the unfettered flow information and ideas, direct result repressive legislation such AIPPA, Criminal Law (Codification and Reform) Act, Interception Communications Act and POSA, among others similar laws, and well the deeply polarised political environment owing the nature the relationship between the ZANU PF-led government and the opposition political parties.
The study further showed that the process was heavily dominated the political parties the Global Political Agreement and all the political parties wanted ensure the adoption constitution that best reflected their preferences and partisan views rather than the will the masses, making the 2013 Constitution elitist negotiated document, contrary the provisions Article the GPA which provided for the right Zimbabweans make constitution for themselves and themselves.

coalition promoting initiative expand Medicaid coverage Montana and big increase tobacco taxes help pay for has filed complaint charging its opponents with illegal campaign activities.
The complaint was filed Monday with the state Commissioner Political Practices Tara Veazey, initiative campaign manager for Healthy Montana, coalition health-care organizations and other groups that pushing Initiative 185, which would raise the state tax cigarettes pack.
Those activities include the failure report expenditures made the campaign against I-185, and the use automated telephone solicitations, commonly known “robocalls,” which are illegal Montana unless live operator first obtains permission deliver the recorded message.
The charges are aimed Montanans Against Tax Hikes, group based Helena, Decision Point Consulting, based Ohio, “and any other now unknown entity committee” that has financially supported the illegal activities.
Commissioner Political Practices Jeff Mangan said the complaint was filed Monday, but still has examined determine whether meets all the requirements accepted his office.
said that decision should made Wednesday.
Veasey said interview Monday that the complaint won’t have any bearing I-185, which will appear the ballot November supporters manage collect verified signatures from least 25,468 registered voters statewide June 22.
She said the complaint was filed force dark-money groups publicly revealed, and let Montanans know who spending money influence elections.
“We just want make sure that’s being disclosed, early and often possible,” she said.
Besides raising state taxes cigarettes pack, I-185 would increase taxes smokeless tobacco percent and create new taxes vaping and products.
also would extend Montana’s expanded Medicaid program, which provides health care more than 94,000 low-income Montanans, past 2019.
Denowh, the former director the Montana Republican Party and president the Montana Group, political consulting firm based Helena, did not return calls seeking comment, but told Yellowstone Public Radio was still reviewing the complaint and had comment.
According the complaint, “several individuals” reported receiving robocalls around May offering them $100 take part focus group.
Veazey says the complaint that when she called the number provided, her call was answered voicemail service for Decision Point Consulting.
“Clearly,” the complaint says, “the hiring out state company produce robocall expenditure,” creating and conducting focus group meeting.
But Decision Point Consulting has not filed C-2, spending disclosure statement with the state.
“If MATH paid for this poll conducted, should have been reported the C-6 disclosure report that was due May 21.
MATH filed form C-6 June but this report failed include any the above-described expenditures,” the complaint said.
That C-6 report, fact, lists only two things: donations totaling $325 and debts $1,700.
“The failure opponents I-185 file any the required financial reports disclose any information about their income sources expenditures represents clear disregard for Montana’s reporting requirements and warrants full and immediate investigation,” the complaint says.

The 2018 Berlin anti-semitic attack was attack street the German capital Berlin.
Two young men wearing Jewish skullcaps were insulted Arabic-speaking passers-by.
One the two, Israeli citizen, was beaten with belt Palestinian attacker.
Video footage led public outrage.
There have been other similar incidents involving Muslim assailants particular.
spokesman for the Jewish community said the attack showed that "Jewish people are not safe here".
The number overall anti-Semitic offenses Berlin has been rising recent years.
2017, police had registered 288 anti-Semitic acts; 2016, 197 anti-Semitic cases had been recorded.
The Research and Information Center Antisemitism Berlin (RIAS) counts total 947 anti-Semitic incidents for 2017, but there may unrecorded cases.
April 2018, Adam Armoush, 21-year-old Israeli Arab living Berlin and his German friend Moroccan origin went through street Prenzlauer Berg taking selfies while wearing kippahs.
According Armoush, was trying win argument with Jewish friends his Israel proving that could walk through Berlin wearing Jewish skullcap.
one point, argument with three Arab-speaking young men started, which was followed violent attack.
Armoush managed document the violence with his cell phone and later allowed the video shared Facebook, from where the video went viral.
The video shows the main attacker, later identified 19-year-old Palestinian from refugee camp Syria who had come Germany war refugee, beat Armoush repeatedly with his belt.
During the onslaught, the attacker shouted the word "Yahudi", the Arabic word for Jew, before one his companions intervened and dragged him away.
According Armoush, the main attacker had also tried hurt him with glass bottle.
consequence the beating, Armoush suffered minor injuries.
Before turning himself police, the main attacker contacted representative the Palestinian student community Berlin and appeared short video that was distributed social media.
They denied was hostile towards Jews that his actions had been motivated anti-Semitism.
The main attacker turned himself accompanied his lawyer the day after the incident.
arrest warrant against him was executed.
Several politicians, among them chancellor Angela Merkel and foreign minister Heiko Maas condemned the attack.
the Israeli television, Merkel warned persistent hatred Jews Germany.
She spoke "different form anti-Semitism" coming from people Arab descent.
After this and other incidents, the President the Central Council Jews Germany, Josef Schuster, warned Jewish men "not show themselves openly with kippah the metropolitan milieu Germany".
Under the slogan "Berlin Wears Kippah" the Jewish community Berlin called all citizens wear the kippah April solidarity with the victims.
"Anti-Semitic crimes increase dramatically Germany".
Jewish Telegraphic Agency.
August 2018.
Retrieved August 2018.
Eddy, Melissa; Curry, Andrew (18 April 2018).
"Fury Germany Rap Duo With Anti-Jewish Lyrics Gets Award".
New York Times.
Retrieved April 2018.
"'Jewish Man' Attacked Berlin Admits He's Israeli-Arab Who Didn't Believe Germany Was anti-Semitic".
Haaretz.
DPA, AP.
April 2018.

There was plenty grumbling about the Advance Report Durable Goods for January 2010 that was released today.
The headline number showed new orders but new orders were actually down 0.6%.
you're overweight tech, like am, though, you would smiling happily.
I'll run through few charts and you'll see why.
Let's start looking shipments for the tech sector whole and than for the sub-sectors computers, semiconductors and communications equipment.
With the great improvement shipments, it's disappointing see Tech lagging other sectors like Materials and Consumer Discretionary.
Some would argue, however, that shipments are the past, and stocks move based future performance.
that's truly the case, Tech might still the place be.
Take look these charts showing new orders.
You can see that new orders are quite strong, again reaching levels last seen 2008.
Though communication equipment lagging the other sub-sectors, too shows increasing orders.
have written several posts (here, here and here) how the tech earnings scorecard this earnings season has been pretty darn strong.
have contended that tech stocks should getting more investor interest.
Today's Durable Goods report may, think, begin convert the unbelievers.
noticed that the NASAQ finished Thursday better shape than the 500 and the Dow.
Are seeing the beginning upsurge tech?

(Prospective) Tenant Life Support!
ran into friend mine recently who helps manage very large portfolio rental properties.
She always has the funniest stories tell, and just had pass this one on.
They had running for rental property nice area town.
was typical bedroom bread and butter house and there had been lot interest this particular property.
She said that until just before closing, she had the usual run the mill tenant applications.
Then the very last minute before closing, lady ran into the office waving cash and said she had put her application today.
She told the property manager that she was going have move quickly she currently lived two story house with the bedrooms the second floor.
The applicant said this house was the right location, and she reiterated that needed least one first floor bedroom.
She said this house would perfect!
The prospective tenant began filling out the application and when was complete my, friend looked over everything noting that the income given was for her and her husband.
Wanting get much information possible, she asked her why she needed move quickly.
This nice lady said that, “Her husband would off life support any day now, and when came home from the hospital was going need first floor bedroom that would accommodate his hospital bed would need for his long recovery”.
After friend recovered from the shock hearing was currently ICU life support, she gently asked the applicant what she would some chance didn’t recover.
The tenant said she was sure was going recover but might take some time.
She just needed house with first floor bedroom.
friend pointed out that she didn’t have sufficient income herself rent the property.
how did they leave it?
friend the property manager told her see she could find co-signer and get back with her.
She never heard back from the lady really don’t know what happened.
Wow, that’s sad situation.
But makes sense that you need rent someone who can pay.
suspect she probably found another home rent hopefully works out for her and her husband (and the landlord).
hope too Mike.
the end the day, you have have tenant that can pay the rent that you can pay for the house.
would much worse let them move (knowing that they couldn’t pay) and then have evict them.
one wants that.
